items.title: Restaurant sector playbook: Stocks to buy & avoid
items.description: Jacob Aiken-Phillips, Melius Research vice president of consumer and retail research, joins Market Domination to explain why he believes McDonald's (MCD) and Starbucks (SBUX) pricing no longer match the value consumers expect. Watch the video above to hear Aiken-Phillips explain why Dutch Bros (BROS) still looks like a Buy. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination here.

items.title: McDonald’s Canada names Annemarie Swijtink CEO and president
items.description: Her appointment follows the retirement of Michèle Boudria, who had been with the company for 40 years

items.title: Domino's Q2 Earnings on Deck: Will New Efforts Deliver a Sales Boost?
items.description: DPZ rides on menu innovation, new delivery partners and loyalty perks in the second quarter, but will weak traffic drag results?

items.title: McDonald's Canada Announces Leadership Transition
items.description: McDonald's Canada today announced that Annemarie Swijtink, currently the Managing Director of McDonald's Netherlands, has been named President & Chief Executive Officer. Michèle Boudria, the current Canadian President & CEO, has announced her retirement after an impressive 40-year career with the company. The leadership changes will take effect on September 1, 2025.

items.title: KeyBanc: Sentiment positive for YUM and SHAK, Investors warming up to CMG, MCD
items.description: Investing.com -- Investor sentiment in the restaurant sector is currently strongest for YUM! Brands and Shake Shack (NYSE:SHAK), according to the latest fieldwork and analysis from KeyBanc Capital Markets.
